title: Create separate zoomrecovery-dev script file
description: There is no zoomrecovery-dev script — the dev formula just installs the same 'zoomrecovery' file. Need a separate zoomrecovery-dev script that beta testers install alongside the stable version, with enhanced recovery logic for Zoom 7.0.0. The formula should do bin.install 'zoomrecovery-dev' so it installs as a distinct binary.
close_reason: Created zoomrecovery-dev script as separate file with sandbox-exec, Apple Silicon support, selective data wipe, full cache/pref cleanup

title: Investigate what Zoom checks for 1132 ban enforcement
description: The current script wipes enc.db files, viper.ini, caches, and spoofs MAC addresses, but this is not enough to clear Error 1132. Need to investigate what identifiers Zoom actually uses to enforce the device ban. Likely candidates: (1) Zoom app installation UUID/identifiers, (2) Keychain entries, (3) LaunchAgent/LaunchDaemon plists, (4) Zoom preferences in ~/Library/Preferences, (5) hardware serial number fingerprinting, (6) Zoom's ZoomID or other persistent identifiers written during installation. The working manual fix was complete Zoom removal + force install of older version, which suggests the ban is tied to something in the app bundle or persistent install-time data.
notes: FINDINGS: Zoom reads IOPlatformSerialNumber, IOPlatformUUID, board-id, IOMACAddress via IOKit, and /Library/Preferences/SystemConfiguration/NetworkInterfaces.plist. MAC spoofing is blocked on Apple Silicon + macOS 14+. The working fix (per PrimeUpYourLife/1132-fixer) is sandbox-exec with a deny profile + full data wipe + DNS flush + Private Wi-Fi Address cycling. Our script only truncates files and does basic MAC spoofing — that is why it fails.

title: zoomrecovery-dev does not resolve Error 1132
description: The current script (v1.2.0-dev.1) fails to actually fix Zoom Error 1132. After intentionally triggering 1132 for testing, running zoomrecovery-dev did NOT resolve the error. Had to manually: (1) completely remove Zoom, (2) force install an older version. This means the current recovery approach (MAC spoofing + wiping enc.db/viper.ini + clearing caches) is insufficient for the actual 1132 ban mechanism.

title: Add complete Zoom uninstall + reinstall to recovery script
description: Since clearing caches/MACs alone doesn't fix 1132, the script needs to: (1) fully uninstall Zoom (remove app bundle, all Library support dirs, preferences, keychain entries, launch agents), (2) install a known-working older version of Zoom. This matches the manual workaround that actually resolved 1132.

title: Sunset zoomrecovery stable and promote zoomrecovery-dev to stable
description: Once zoomrecovery-dev is confirmed working on Zoom 7.0.0+, sunset the old zoomrecovery script and promote the dev version to become the new stable release. Steps: (1) Confirm zoomrecovery-dev fixes 1132 on Zoom 7.0.0+ via beta testing, (2) Add deprecation notice to old zoomrecovery (print warning on run that it will be removed), (3) Rename zoomrecovery-dev to zoomrecovery with the sandbox approach, (4) Update Homebrew stable formula to point to new version, (5) Remove/archive the old formula, (6) Update README to reflect the transition. Zoom 6.x is EOL territory — once most users are on 7.0.0+ the old script has no audience.

value: zoomrecovery (stable) works correctly on Zoom versions below 7.0.0. Verified working on 6.7.7 as of 2026-04-29. zoomrecovery-dev is the new script targeting Zoom 7.0.0+ with sandbox-exec. The two scripts coexist — stable uses MAC spoof + file wipe, dev uses sandbox identity masking + full cleanup.

Zoom 1132 ban enforcement: Zoom reads IOPlatformSerialNumber, IOPlatformUUID, board-id, IOMACAddress via IOKit, and NetworkInterfaces.plist. MAC spoofing alone is insufficient on Apple Silicon + macOS 14+ because direct MAC spoofing is blocked. The working approach (from PrimeUpYourLife/1132-fixer) is: (1) full rm -rf of Zoom data/caches/prefs/savedState, (2) defaults delete us.zoom.xos, (3) kill updater processes and bootout launch agents, (4) flush DNS, (5) launch Zoom inside sandbox-exec with a profile that denies reading hardware identifiers, (6) cycle Private Wi-Fi Address on Apple Silicon. Our script only truncates files and tries basic MAC spoofing which is why it fails.

zoomrecovery-dev v1.2.0-dev.1 does NOT fix Error 1132 in practice. Manual workaround that works: complete Zoom removal + force install of older version. Current script approach (MAC spoof + cache/db wipe) is insufficient. The 1132 ban mechanism checks something beyond what the script currently clears.
